safety_overall: All candidates fail dual-criterion mixing requirements with high Damköhler numbers and mixing ratios. Candidates 14 and 17 show better conversion but still require mixing improvements. All candidates show mixing limitations with hard gate failures, but achieve target intensification. Candidates 24 and 27 have impractically long coils requiring segmentation. All candidates show severe mixing limitations with Da_mass >> 1 and r_mix >> 0.20, indicating poor radial mixing that could compromise reaction uniformity and safety. All candidates have dual-criterion mixing failures with high Damköhler numbers, indicating mass transfer limitations. Candidates 43 and 1 also have impractically long coils requiring segmentation. All candidates suffer from severe mixing limitations with Da_mass >> 1 and r_mix >> 0.20, indicating mass transfer controlled reactions. The 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition will be significantly hindered by poor mixing in these configurations. Candidate 5 shows severe mixing limitations with both mass transfer Damköhler number (51.18) and mixing ratio (1.89) exceeding acceptable thresholds, creating a hard gate failure.
